title: Who will moderate the ABC debate between Harris, Trump?
shortDescription: Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are set to debate each other on ABC Sep. 10. Here's what to know about moderators Linsey Davis and David Muir.

title: Tim Walz has no stocks, no bonds and doesn't own a home
shortDescription: Tim Walz's personal finances are an anomaly in US presidential politics: He has no stocks, bonds, or private equity, and he doesn't even own a home.

title: Kamala Harris and Donald Trump will debate in September | The Excerpt
shortDescription: Plus: Trump campaign goes after Tim Walz's military history. How many Americans admit to shoplifting? Giant pandas are on display at San Diego Zoo.

title: Aside from promoting her book, Nancy Pelosi is running for reelection
shortDescription: Nancy Pelosi is busy promoting her new book, but she will also run for reelection in November.

title: Harris 'glad' to debate Trump in September
shortDescription: Donald Trump has proposed two additional debate dates, which Kamala Harris says she is happy to discuss further.

title: Patriotism, polarization, and pommel horse: Olympics and the election
shortDescription: For nearly three weeks, the world watches in awe at the summer Olympic games in Paris. But back home, Americans are more polarized than ever.

title: MLK’s son talks Sonya Massey, Trump & Harris, America today
shortDescription: Son of the civil rights icon and his wife Arndrea Waters King sat down with USA TODAY to share their thoughts on today's issues.

title: Nancy Pelosi gets candid about Joe Biden
shortDescription: Here is what Former Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi had to say about President Joe Biden.

title: Swing state election board's Republican members make Trump happy again
shortDescription: Donald Trump offered his seal of approval to Georgia election board Republicans for the second time in a week, as they reopened an investigation.

title: Trump compares Jan. 6 crowd size to MLK march
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump's comparison of the Jan. 6 rally crowd to Martin Luther King's March on Washington has raised ire on social media.

title: Trump lashes out at Harris, Biden: 5 takeaways from his Mar-a-Lago press conference
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump lashed out at Vice President Kamala Harris and President Joe Biden and pledged there will be a peaceful transfer of power even if he loses the 2024 election.

title: 'Illegal immigrants' can't vote, get licenses in Florida | Fact check
shortDescription: There's no evidence the video shows "illegal immigrants." People living in the country illegally can't obtain driver's licenses or vote in Florida.

title: Video shows delays after police spotted Trump shooter
shortDescription: Video released Thursday by Butler Township reveals the crucial moments before former President Donald Trump was shot on July 13.

title: Trump and Harris agree to ABC debate on Sept. 10
shortDescription: Donald Trump and Kamala Harris say they're ready to square off at a Sept. 10 debate to be moderated by ABC News.

title: Harris shows overall lead but Trump gains with his policies
shortDescription: Harris edges out Trump in latest poll, marking a 4-point shift from late July, following Biden's exit from the race and Harris' entrance.

title: Officers who protected the Capitol on Jan. 6 will campaign for Harris
shortDescription: Three officers who defended the Capitol from rioters on Jan. 6, 2021, are campaigning for Vice President Kamala Harris.

title: Trump said he agreed with Walz's response to riots after Floyd murder
shortDescription: Donald Trump has criticized Tim Walz's response to George Floyd riots on the campaign trail, but the former president praised his efforts in 2020.

title: When is Trump's news conference?
shortDescription: The Republican presidential nominee holds a press conference at Mar-a-Lago on Thursday.

title: Will voters hear Jan. 6 Trump trial evidence before Election Day?
shortDescription: Proposals due Friday in former President Donald Trump's federal Jan. 6 case will shed light on whether voters will hear evidence before Election Day.
